programmes.show_description: The Envious Court: Frank and Lu investigate a death threat at Runningbrook, an exclusive tennis club which hides a toxic secret.
Soft Touch: An involving and emotional mystery for Andy and Peter. Dalziel and Pascoe probe the murder of a successful factory owner, and discover a tormented family.
Stewart Wilken: Micki arrives in Port Elizabeth to testify in the trial of the gruesome 'cannibal killer' Stewart Wilken - and she's also desperate to interview him.
Old Friends: Sitcom with those Essex sisters. Tracey invites an old schoolfriend to stay in the guest room. As the pair relive their past adventures, Sharon begins to feel deserted.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Sweet Smell of Success: Sitcom with Chigwell's most famous sisters. In an effort to raise some cash, Tracey thinks about selling Darryl's old car. Sharon becomes a saleswoman.
Muesli: Drop in for a cuppa with the Essex sisters. Tracey decides to experience Darryl's ordeal behind bars by creating her own prison in the attic, with Sharon as her cellmate.
Keep Off the Grass: The classic Essex girls sitcom. Tracey and Sharon are faced with a moral dilemma when they witness the getaway of a criminal they know after a hold-up.
Black Virgin of Vladimir - Part Two: Second of a two-parter with that scoundrel of the antique showrooms. Lovejoy tries to recover money from an unscrupulous antiques dealer.
Friends in High Places: Lighthearted drama with the raffish antiques dealer. After six months in Spain, Lovejoy returns to renew old friendships and start a new romance!
Out to Lunch: Emotions cloud the wily antique dealer's judgements in the popular drama. As Lovejoy falls for Lady Victoria, he buys some apparently fake watercolours.
The Tanganyika Green: GK Chesterton's sleuthing padre gets involved once again to investigate the murder of a Colonial Postal Service officer, when the fair comes to Kembleford.
Good Morning Lemmings: Another involving case for the veteran detective team comprised of some of the UK's favourite actors. The team probes the death of a graffiti artist.
Fashion Victim: A glamorous case for the veteran detectives in the popular crime caper. The wife of a murdered fashion designer persuades the team to reinvestigate the case.
Mother Redcap: Alan Davies and Caroline Quentin team up to solve another baffling mystery. A judge is found stabbed to death in a room that nobody else could have entered.
Death on the Close Part 1: When a man with no ID or connection to Marlow is found dead in the middle of a suburban road, Judith, Becks and Suzie investigate.
Death on the Close Part 2: A link to a past missing persons case shines a new light on the residents of Linnet Close - but could they be responsible for murder on their doorstep?
Nun of the Above: When a local nun is murdered, the team investigates a convent where all the sisters have secrets and nothing is what it seems.
Another torso is washed up from the Thames, this time at Putney Bridge, and Llewellyn finds traces of Spanish Fly, an aphrodisiac used by the Marquis de Sade.
If Wishes Were Horses: Another absorbing case for the sleuths from two ends of the social spectrum. As Lynley looks forward to fatherhood, his wife Helen helps him with a case.
Good Morning Lemmings: Another involving case for the veteran detective team comprised of some of the UK's favourite actors. The team probes the death of a graffiti artist.
Lights, Camera, Murder!: A TV crew arrives to use St Vincent's Convent as a location for a spy thriller; the filming becomes all too real when a live gun is fired instead of a replica.
Sweet Smell of Success: Sitcom with Chigwell's most famous sisters. In an effort to raise some cash, Tracey thinks about selling Darryl's old car. Sharon becomes a saleswoman.
Death by Vinyl: First Chapter: Members of Cat's old band reunite to record an album at a retreat not far from Wildermarsh - and it's not long before one of them turns up dead.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Love and War: Part 2: Quinnan's secret comes out, dividing Sun Hill. Crime fighting takes a back seat as Quinnan and Garfield clash.
Hot Money: Burnside and Meadows take a gamble when they set up an undercover operation in a local casino.
Crash Landing: A plane makes a crash-landing in Sun Hill, plunging Daly into an investigation in which his ex-lover plays a crucial role.
Father's Day: Boyden's estranged daughter asks him for protection. Boyden helps her out and they start to rekindle their relationship.
Sweet Sixteen: Skase feels he is the laughing stock of CID when he is abducted and robbed by a gang of teenage girls.
Nonnatus House comes under new management in the shape of the formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter), who begins making some drastic changes to the clinic.
Sister Ursula insists the midwives do not become emotionally involved with their patients, but Patsy faces a hard decision when she meets an expectant couple with dwarfism.
An emotional adaptation of Catherine Cookson's best-selling 1920s novel. Bridget McQueen and her new husband, a black seaman, are faced with bigotry on all sides.
Richard Armitage stars in the epic four-part adaptation of Elizabeth Gaskell's classic novel. Margaret Hale and her parents are forced to move to the town of Milton.
Richard Armitage stars in the second part of the lavish adaptation of Elizabeth Gaskell's classic novel. Tensions rise as the workers strike. Margaret tells Bessy a secret.
And Then There Were Nun: As Great Slaughter's Quincentennial Celebrations near, Sister Helena is found dead in the chapel, having been bludgeoned over the head.
One of Our Own: Feature-length forensics mystery for Dr Nikki Alexander and team in the long-running crime drama. The body of a police officer is found in his burnt-out car.
